questions.question: Derive the mean and variance of the Poisson distribution.
keyPoints: Start with E[X] = Σk·P(X=k) from k=0 to ∞
Substitute Poisson PMF, cancel k with k!
Factor out λ·e^(-λ), use substitution j = k-1
For variance: derive E[X²] using E[X(X-1)] + E[X]

questions.question: Derive the mean of the Normal distribution using the standard substitution method.
keyPoints: Substitute z = (x-μ)/σ, so x = μ + σz
Split into two integrals: μ·∫e^(-z²/2)dz + σ·∫z·e^(-z²/2)dz

questions.question: A web server receives an average of 5 requests per second. Find the probability of receiving exactly 3 requests in a given second.
State the properties of the Normal distribution. Find the probability that X lies between μ-σ and μ+σ for a standard normal distribution.
keyPoints: Symmetric about mean μ, bell-shaped curve
Standard normal: Z = (X-μ)/σ with μ=0, σ=1

questions.question: Explain the relationship between Poisson and Normal distributions. Under what conditions can a Poisson distribution be approximated by a Normal distribution?
keyPoints: For large λ (typically λ > 20), Poisson ≈ Normal(μ=λ, σ²=λ)
Poisson is discrete, Normal is continuous — use continuity correction
Both are used extensively in CS: network traffic, error rates
